item.title: DGHS corrects measles data
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services on Sunday updated its measles death and infection data by incorporating previously unreported deaths and infections, a day after New Age published a report highlighting major discrepancies in official figures....Health & Wellness

item.title: Former Lt Gen Masud was behind 2007-08 inhuman acts, ICT told
item.description: The chief prosecutor of the International Crimes Tribunal on Sunday alleged that retired lieutenant general Masud Uddin Chowdhury was the ‘main mastermind’ behind the inhuman acts and crimes against humanity committed during the military-backed caretaker government......

item.title: Govt mulls overtime pay, promotion for constables
item.description: The government is considering overtime allowances for the ranks from constable to inspector for performing additional duty beyond regular hours and honorary promotions for constables, assistant sub-inspectors and sub-inspectors as many lower rank officers......

item.title: ICT charges Taposh, Nanak, 26 others
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Sunday indicted 24 Awami League leaders and four police senior officers over the killing of nine protesters in the capital’s Mohammadpur area during the 2024 mass uprising....

item.title: Potatoes rot in north for storage crisis
item.description: Potato farmers in northern districts of the country are dumping their produce in rotten condition due to inadequate storage facilities and falling prices caused by surplus production....

item.title: Lightning deaths persist as steps to calm aerial rage fail
item.description: Fifteen-year old Akhi Akter was helping her parents in saving their newly harvested paddy from getting wet on their courtyard as thunderclouds roared in the sky Sunday afternoon. ...

item.description: The Brand Practitioners Bangladesh hosted the Health and Well-being Marketing Fest 3.0 with the theme ‘healthier world through smarter marketing’ at a hotel in Dhaka on Saturday....Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Structural challenges in university admission
item.description: UNIVERSITY admission is a defining phase in the lives of students in Bangladesh — one that tests not only academic ability and readiness for higher education, but also patience, financial capacity, and mental endurance. Every year, hundreds of......

item.title: Pathway out of electricity crisis
item.description: THE Sun remains the Earth’s oldest and most dependable source of energy. Without sunlight, life itself would not exist. Every day, the world receives immense amounts of heat and light from the Sun, and modern technology now allows this energy to......

item.title: Importance of research in higher education
item.description: AS OF early 2026, Bangladesh has 55 public universities and over 116 private universities. Total university student enrolment exceeds one million in dedicated undergraduate and graduate programmes, while the National University (affiliated colleges)......

item.title: Rethinking garment export strategy
item.description: BANGLADESH’S garment industry has long been central to the country’s economy. It has shaped export earnings, created millions of jobs, and underpinned decades of industrial growth. What began as a low-cost manufacturing base has evolved......Geographic Reference

item.title: Politics of ‘infiltrator’
item.description: WHEN I try to understand why Bengal’s arguments about ‘outsiders’ feel both intimate and cruel, my mind returns to Bheramara — my childhood town — where the border was never only a line on a map. It was a presence in the air: in the way......

item.title: Robust recovery, regulatory framework needed for NBFIs
item.description: THE disclosure that nearly the entire loan portfolios of at least eight non-bank financial institutions and more than a half of 30 other NBFIs have turned defaulted shows a catastrophic collapse of governance in the sector. Recent Bangladesh Bank data.....

item.title: Govt must fix measles reporting failures
item.description: THE allegations that have come up against the Directorate General of Health Services for under-reporting measles deaths and infections are worrying on a couple of counts. Firstly, the under-reporting of measles cases weakens the government’s......

item.title: TCB to begin 10-day Eid-ul-Azha truck sales today
item.description: The Trading Corporation of Bangladesh will resume its nationwide truck sale programme today to provide essential commodities at subsidised prices ahead of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Barca claim La Liga title with Clasico win over Real Madrid
item.description: Barcelona secured their 29th Spanish league title after beating Real Madrid 2-0 in the Clasico on Sunday. Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres struck in the first half to help Hansi Flick's side clinch La Liga in consecutive years, while consigning Madrid to a trophyless season. ...

item.title: Arsenal on the brink of Premier League title
item.description:
Arsenal are on the brink of a first Premier League title in 22 years after surviving a hugely contentious finale to beat West Ham 1-0, while Aston Villa were held 2-2 at Burnley on Sunday.
...

item.title: JU signs MoU with Hubei University for research, innovation
item.description: Jahangirnagar University signed a memorandum of understanding with China’s Hubei University on Sunday to strengthen cooperation in education, research and digital innovation initiatives, university officials said....

item.title: Rail accident kills truck driver in Jashore
item.description: A coal laden truck driver was killed on the spot as the Rajshahi-bound Mahananda express train hit the truck while it was crossing an unauthorised rail crossing at Nawapara industrial town in Jashore....

item.title: 18 hawkers arrested over attack on Ansar personnel at Savar
item.description: The police in Savar have filed a case over an alleged attack by hawkers that left six Ansar members injured, and arrested 18 people in connection with the incident, police said on Sunday....

item.title: Five ex-Janata Bank officials jailed for 8 years
item.description: A Chattogram court on Sunday sentenced five former Janata Bank officials of Sheikh Mujib Road Corporate Branch to 8 years imprisonment for embezzling Tk 3.39 crore in collusion with customers 26 years ago....

item.title: ULAB secures 36th position globally in WURI ranking
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh has secured 36th place among the top 100 universities in the World University Rankings for Innovation 2026....Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Russian Victory Day celebrated in Dhaka
item.description: The Russian embassy in Bangladesh has celebrated the 81st anniversary of Victory in the Great Patriotic War through commemorative events, cultural performances and a motorcycle rally in the capital, honouring veterans and the historic defeat of Nazi Germany....

item.title: NCP names 100 candidates
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Sunday announced the names of 100 preliminary candidates for upazila parishad and municipality elections as part of its preparation for the upcoming local government polls in the country....

item.title: Two held over harassment of foreign tourists at Natore park
item.description: The police on Sunday arrested two youths in connection with a case filed over the harassment of two foreign nationals at a park at Lalpur upazila in Natore....

item.title: Barishal Univ teachers to start shutdown today for promotion
item.description: The teachers of Barishal University on Sunday declared a complete academic and administrative shutdown again from today for promotion....

item.title: Housing co former DMD arrested over Tk 120cr fraud
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department early Sunday arrested a former deputy managing director of Holistic Home Builders Limited, a real estate company, in connection with a case filed over embezzling about Tk 120 crore from about 470 customers....

item.description: A case has been filed over the killing of five members of a family by slitting their throats in Gazipur with the prime accused absconding....

item.title: Ukraine reports multiple casualties on 2nd day of Russia truce
item.description: Russian attacks wounded at least nine people in Ukraine on Sunday, the second day of what was supposed to have been a three-day truce with Russia, Ukrainian officials said....

item.title: Bank resolution not to be lender driven, minister
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Sunday said that the government would implement the Bank Resolution Act 2026 according to its own plans, not under conditions imposed by lenders....

item.title: Women in 6pc mid-management posts, 22pc at entry-level: report
item.description: A recent study has found sharp gaps between the percentage of women holding entry-level positions and that holding mid-level management positions in the country’s private sector jobs....

item.title: Depositors demand return of savings from merged banks
item.description: Depositors of five crisis-hit merged Islamic banks formed a human chain in Rajshahi on Sunday, demanding the return of their savings, restoration of normal banking services and cancellation of a newly introduced ‘haircut’ policy that allegedly restricts......

item.title: 5 BNP, Jamaat activists briefly detained
item.description: The police have detained five activists of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami from Mirpur in Kushtia briefly for their alleged involvement in creating pressure on the police to release a suspected drug peddler from Mirpur police station....Diaspora Community Forum

item.description: Leaders of the Samajtantrik Chhatra Front, the student wing of the Socialist Party of Bangladesh, Barishal metropolitan unit, on Sunday demanded the allocation of 25 per cent of the development budget for the education sector in upcoming budget of the 2026-27 fiscal year....

item.description: The police in Sharsha upazila in Jashore on Saturday recovered the body of 26-year-old Iqramul Kabir after arresting four people in this connection....

item.title: Rajshahi mangoes hit market May 15, Naogaon May 22
item.description: Authorities in the country’s major mango-producing districts – Rajshahi and Naogaon – announced separate harvesting schedules on Sunday for the current season in a bid to prevent immature fruits from entering the market and ensure quality produce for consumers....

item.description: THE police recovered the hanging bodies of two housewives from separate areas in Meherpur’s Mujibnagar and Gangni upazilas on Sunday....

item.title: Youths must learn ethics alongside digital literacy: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Sunday said that the future generation must be educated in moral values alongside digital literacy in the current era of artificial intelligence and technology....

item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion has arrested a man with a shotgun and ammunition, allegedly looted from a police station during the August 2024politicalchangeover, in Narayanganj’s Fatullah area....

item.title: Govt keen on ensuring edn-friendly condition for Rohingya children: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: The state minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj on Sunday said that the government was intently working to ensure a safe, humane and education-friendly environment for Rohingya children....

item.title: Govt warns unregistered agencies on trafficking
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism ministry on Sunday issued an urgent warning against unregistered and expired travel agencies amid a human trafficking allegation, urging travellers to verify agency licenses before making financial transactions....

item.title: DGFI ex-official Manjil sent to jail after remand
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday sent to jail former Directorate General of Forces Intelligence official retired Major Manjil Haider Chowdhury in a case filed over the killing of garment worker Rubel in the capital’s Adabor Ring Road area during the July mass uprising....City & Local Guides

item.title: Bangladesh, Italy seek closer migration ties
item.description: Bangladesh and Italy on Sunday discussed expanding ongoing cooperation to ensure safer and regular migration management....Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Sylhet nat’l minority women exposed to crisis: study
item.description: A severe lack of awareness about reproductive health along with deep-rooted social and religious stigma around it has been found among the marginalised national minority communities in Sylhet in a recent study by the Shahjalal University of Science and Technology....

item.description: BRAC University has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with the Anhui Normal University to promote academic and institutional collaboration and joint research across a wide range of disciplines....
